observations.recommendation:
1.7	We recommended that Management:
require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of physical inventory taking and counting earlier in order to provide sufficient time for reconciliation purposes;
instruct the Municipal GSO and Municipal Accounting Office to undertake continuous reconciliation between the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records and adjust them accordingly, as necessary, to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts in order to fairly present the PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as of year-end; and
instruct the Municipal GSO and Municipal Accounting Office to undertake collaborative procedures, such as assigning personnel tasked primarily to reconcile accounts to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and to study the applicability of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to facilitate the fair presentation of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.

2.6	We recommended that the Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to appropriately and consistently record the transactions undertaken by the Municipality using appropriate accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements as of year-end.

3.9	We recommended that Management:
a.	require the OIC – Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in CY 2024 to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” in the Trust Fund books in strict compliance to applicable provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
b.	instruct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to identify the cause of discrepancies between their records and the balances of Continuing Appropriations presented in the Notes to the Financial Statements through constant monthly reconciliation and draw the necessary adjustments to correct any errors or inconsistencies; and
c.	comply with the proforma report of LDRRMF Utilization Report as per FDP Form 8/Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 to determine the details of the PPAs from the prior years which includes the balances from prior years.

4.8	We recommended that the Management, going forward, distinguish capitalizable repairs and maintenance from routinary ones in order to identify expenditures which should be recognized as assets in faithful compliance to IPSAS 1, 3 and 17 in order to present fairly the Municipality’s financial statements as of year-end.
4.9	Moreover, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to draw the necessary adjusting entry to rectify the misstatements caused by the non-recognition of the cost of aforementioned repairs as assets, to present fairly the accounts presented in the financial statements.

5.6	We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a.	review the transactions charged against the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and draw the necessary adjusting entries to properly record the transactions and present fairly the accounts presented in the financial statements; and
b.	moving forward, refer to the proper classification of accounts in adherence with the RCA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to enhance the transparency and integrity of the financial statements.

6.5	We recommended that the Management direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly secure the monthly BS from the authorized government depository bank for all their bank accounts maintained and subsequently prepare the corresponding monthly BRS in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1455 and Section 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to establish the correct balances to be presented in the financial statements.
6.6	Moreover, we recommended that the Management consider the hiring of additional personnel to work on preparing the monthly bank reconciliation of the Municipality, among others, to ease up the work load of personnel involved in monitoring other accounts and financial transactions.

7.6	We recommend that Management instruct the OIC – Municipal Accountant and other offices concerned to undertake concerted effort to determine the details of the beginning balance of the Accounts Payable and reconstruct the necessary schedules and ledgers in adherence to Sections 71(2) and 111 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1.

8.6	We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to gather sufficient information from available records and perform other necessary procedures that would help reconstruct the required schedules and ledgers in strict adherence to Section 111 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1.

9.8	We recommend that the Management, through the Municipal GSO and Inventory Committee, report all unserviceable properties in the IIRUSP and IIRUP and submit the same to the Audit Team in order to facilitate their disposal through the appropriate mode of disposal as determined by the Inventory Committee in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

10.6	We recommend that the Management:
a.	ensure that all information required in the trip tickets are completely indicated thereat in order to establish the validity of the said document in strict compliance to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of Administrative Order No. 239 dated September 15, 2008; and
b.	adopt proper controls on fuel consumption and conduct briefing for all employees concerned on the preparation and use of trip tickets as the primary document to ensure that the travels are authorized.

11.7	We nevertheless recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the remaining PPAs to optimally utilize the available funds and ensure the achievement of desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality, in compliance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

12.5	We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to submit the aforementioned lacking documentary requirements in conformity with relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to enable the Audit Team to conduct proper evaluation of the subject transactions.
12.7   We further recommended that the Management, moving forward, instruct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to ensure that all documentary requirements have been secured and attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing of payments to suppliers in strict compliance to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

14.5	We recommended that the Management, through the Bids and Awards Committee, ensure that Request for Quotation Forms and Purchase Orders for PPAs whose cost exceed P50,000.00 and qualifies for alternative modes of procurement are posted at the PhilGEPS website and at conspicuous places in strict compliance to Sections E and L (2) of Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management, through the Bids and Awards Committee, strictly observe relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the consolidation of procurements to allow the Municipality to negotiate better deals with suppliers through bulk purchasing and avail of discounts and savings.

16.6	We recommended that Management ensure strict compliance with the withholding and remittance of taxes, mandatory government deductions and contributions by promptly remitting intact all withheld funds and contributions during the month to the appropriate government agencies by the ensuing month, in faithful compliance with Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to trace all fund transfers received from all NGAs, GOCCs, and LGUs in the previous years’ using the collection records and other relative documents and provide the Municipal Accountant the list and details of the fund transfers.
Require the Municipal Accountant to send inquiry/confirmation letters to the concerned NGAs, GOCCs and LGUs based on the list given by the Municipal Treasurer to establish existence of payables of LGU due to the concerned agencies/creditors.
Provide adequate disclosures in the notes to financial statement as to the condition/status of the above payable accounts and the reason for the absence of subsidiary ledger/supporting schedule for the information of the users of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances of the concerned officers and employees, otherwise, withhold the salaries of these officers and employees until they have settled their cash advance/s, pursuant to Items 5.1.3 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to further intensify tax campaign activities in order to collect the RPT and SET Receivables, pursuant to Section 256 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that collectors be reminded of their obligation to turn over all their collections intact at the end of every business day to the Treasurer/Liquidating Officer. Failure to do so may be a ground for relieving them from their duties and responsibilities as collectors of the taxes and other income for municipality.

In addition, the  Municipal Treasurer is advised to adopt the following control measures to ensure that all collections  are turned over by RCOs at the end of every business day;  (a)  verify DSCAF of collectors,   (b) check the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, ( c) make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller and check the same to the new balances on hand column,  (d) indicate in the verification copy of the last official receipt issued by accountable officers, and  (e)  count the money turned over by the collector   and sign the certification and receipt portion of all copies of the DSCAF.
observations.managementAction: All collectors were reminded by management to remit/turn over daily all their collections to the Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommend that management exert effort to locate documents pertaining to the construction of the Iguig Water System as basis for reclassifying the Construction In Progress – Artesian Wells, Reservoirs, Pumping Station and Conduits account to its appropriate fixed asset account.
observations.managementAction: Management has exerted efforts to locate the documents but failed to find the pertinent vouchers except for the documents relating to the loan granted for the  constructing the pumping station.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management to demand immediate liquidation of fund releases as embodied in the Memorandum of Agreement and to strictly comply the procedures for the availment, release and utilization of funds released to NGOs/POs as provided for under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.  The selection, screening and accreditation of NGOs should be undertaken by the committee created by the municipality for the purpose, irrespective of the source of fund.
observations.managementAction:
The municipality complied with the recommendation to demand liquidation of these fund releases from NGOs concerned by sending demand letters thru registered mail but there was no response from concerned NGOs. Management explained that  concerned NGOs have  fulfilled their obligations as agreed in  their respective MOA viz;  Focus on Development Goal Foundation Inc.-  conducted  livelihood trainings and delivered  livelihood kits   distributed to beneficiaries,    and Ginuntuang Alay sa Magsasaka Foundation Inc. – for  full delivery of 193 sets of Organic farm yield packages which were distributed to farmers.
Management assured to observe the procedures in availment, release & utilization of funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under COA Circular 2007-001.
